# Chapter 5 — Results & Testing (CamTraffic)

**Thesis:** Design and Development of an AI-Based Traffic Sign Detection and Traffic Law Enforcement System in Cambodia

Source: `docs/thesis_evidence/AI-06/metrics_summary.json`

| Model | `ai/weights/best.pt` |
| Training run | `ai\weights\camtraffic_signs3` |
| Epochs | 30 |
| Precision | 76.4% |
| Recall | 78.0% |
| **mAP@0.5** | **90.6%** |
| mAP@0.5:0.95 | 88.1% |
| Train images | 131 |
| Val images | 24 |
| Classes (training subset) | 19 |

## 5.2 Held-Out Sign Accuracy (TS-03)

Source: `docs/thesis_evidence/TS-03/accuracy_summary.json`

| Split | Images | Correct | Accuracy | Avg confidence |
|-------|--------|---------|----------|----------------|
| Held-out val (generic filenames) | 209 | 12 | 5.7% | 19.0% |
| Combined | 209 | 12 | 5.7% | 19.0% |

**Interpretation:** mAP@0.5 on the **19-class validation set** is high (90.6%). TS-03 uses **236-class catalog images** with generic filenames — a harder generalization test. Production uses hybrid YOLO + catalog lookup + optional Gemini fallback.

Full results: `docs/thesis_evidence/TS-03/accuracy_results.csv`

| **E2E pipeline** | **28** | **Pass** |
| **Full backend suite** | **130** | **Pass** |

```bash
cd backend && python manage.py test -v 1
python scripts/run_defense_integration.py --full-tests
```

Defense assets (screenshots + slides):

```bash
python scripts/capture_defense_screenshots.py   # admin UI → docs/screenshots/
python scripts/generate_mobile_mockup.py        # driver app mockup (slide 14)
python scripts/generate_defense_slides.py       # docs/CamTraffic_Defense_Slides.pptx
```

**Note:** Screenshot capture uses `domcontentloaded` + UI selectors (not `networkidle`) because the Traffic Signs page lazy-loads 236 images.

## 5.5 Pipeline Performance (Live Demo)

| Stage | Typical latency (CPU) |
|-------|----------------------|
| Image upload → API | < 0.5 s |
| YOLO sign detection | 1–2 s |
| Vehicle detection | 0.5–1 s |
| Plate OCR | 0.5–1 s |
| Violation evaluate + DB save | < 0.3 s |
| **End-to-end** | **< 3 s** |

Webcam loop: ~500 ms capture interval with stabilization (`useWebcamDetection.ts`).

## 5.6 Functional Requirements — Summary

| Requirement | Result |
|-------------|--------|
| Real-time sign detection | ✅ Webcam + upload |
| Hybrid AI (YOLO + Gemini) | ✅ Implemented |
| Vehicle + plate detection | ✅ On detect API |
| Violation rule engine | ✅ 10+ rule types |
| Evidence capture | ✅ Auto frame + crops |
| Khmer + English UI/TTS | ✅ 236 signs |
| Admin dashboard | ✅ Stats + management |
| Fine PDF export | ✅ ReportLab |
| Dashboard analytics PDF | ✅ Reports page → Export PDF |
| Monthly Excel export | ✅ Violations + fines (`openpyxl`) |
| OCR province lookup | ✅ Plate digits → province EN/KM |
| Detection history | ✅ AI Logs page (+ CSV export) |
| Defense slides | ✅ PPTX + app screenshots + mobile mockup |

---

## 5.7 Limitations & Future Work

1. **OCR:** Latin-script plates only; Khmer-script plates need custom OCR training. Province name lookup from leading digits is implemented (codes 1–25).
2. **Vehicle tracking:** ByteTrack integrated for live webcam sessions (`track_session` API + `VehicleTrackingLog` table).
3. **236-class YOLO:** Current `best.pt` covers 19 enforcement-critical classes; full catalog relies on hybrid fallback.
4. **Mobile app:** Responsive web + **slide mockup** (alert → violation → fine); native FCM push is future work.
5. **Spatial ROI violations:** Rule engine uses sign + action pairs; camera ROI zones not yet enforced.

---

## 5.8 Conclusion (Draft Points)

CamTraffic successfully integrates computer vision (YOLOv8), hybrid AI (Gemini fallback), OCR with province lookup, a rule-based expert system for violations, and a bilingual web dashboard for Cambodian traffic enforcement. The system demonstrates a complete pipeline from camera input to digital fine records, with **90.6% mAP@0.5** on the trained sign subset and **130/130 passing backend tests** validating the defense demo flow.
